description: Fix resource leaks, missing error handling, and unguarded error paths in the MCP server discovered during a comprehensive backend code audit.

userStories.title: Fix unbounded endpointCallTimestamps map growth
userStories.description: As a server operator, I want the rate-limit timestamp map to not grow unboundedly so that long-running servers do not leak memory.
acceptanceCriteria: Stale endpoint entries (no calls in the last 60 seconds) are removed from state.endpointCallTimestamps during the rate limit check or via periodic cleanup
A unit test verifies that endpoints with only expired timestamps are pruned from the map
userStories.notes: In platform/mcp-server/src/http-routes.ts at line 135-145, the checkEndpointRateLimit function filters expired timestamps per-endpoint but never removes endpoints whose timestamp arrays are empty after filtering. Over a long server lifetime, endpoints that were called once but never again accumulate as empty arrays in the map. Fix: after filtering timestamps, if the resulting array is empty, delete the endpoint key from the map instead of storing the empty array back. This is a one-line fix inside checkEndpointRateLimit — after the filter on line 137, check if the filtered array is empty and delete the key if so. Also check the set-after-push path on line 142-143.

userStories.title: Add error handling to setTimeout sendExtensionReload calls
userStories.description: As a server operator, I want unhandled errors in delayed sendExtensionReload calls to be caught and logged so that the server does not crash silently.
acceptanceCriteria: All three setTimeout(() => sendExtensionReload(state), 500) call sites wrap the call in a try-catch or use a .catch() pattern
Errors are logged via log.warn or log.error, not silently swallowed

userStories.title: Add error handler for piped ReadableStream in server-node.ts
userStories.description: As a server operator, I want stream errors during HTTP response piping to be handled so that they don't become unhandled error events.
acceptanceCriteria: An 'error' event handler is registered on the nodeStream returned by Readable.fromWeb in the sendResponse function
The error handler destroys the response and logs a warning
